# Define normalization functions
import re, unicodedata

arabic_diacritics = re.compile(r'[\u0610-\u061A\u064B-\u065F\u06D6-\u06ED]')
tatweel = '\u0640'
punct = re.compile(r'[^\w\s]', flags=re.UNICODE)  # but this will remove Arabic letters? \w includes Arabic? Might include numbers. Better to make own.
# We'll implement a normalization function carefully.

def normalize_arabic(s):
    if s is None:
        return ""
    # strip tashkeel (diacritics) and other combining marks
    # Use unicode category to remove characters of category Mn (Mark, nonspacing)
    s = ''.join(ch for ch in s if unicodedata.category(ch) != 'Mn')
    # remove tatweel
    s = s.replace(tatweel, '')
    # unify hamza: map various alef-hamza to bare alef? Developer said unify hamza; ambiguous.
    # We'll map آ أ إ ء to ا (simple unification)
    s = s.replace('آ', 'ا').replace('أ', 'ا').replace('إ', 'ا').replace('ٱ', 'ا').replace('ء','ا')
    # remove punctuation and non-Arabic letters/digits
    # Keep Arabic letters and numbers and space and percent maybe. We'll remove punctuation marks like ،؛؟.:/[]{}()-—... and special characters.
    s = re.sub(r'[^\u0600-\u06FF0-9\s]', ' ', s)
    # collapse multiple spaces
    s = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', s).strip()
    # lowercase not applicable
    return s

# test normalize
test = "وَإِنْ تَسْكُنُوا إِلَى الْأَرْضِ فَاسْأَلُوا مَا أَسْكَنَتْكُمْ"

    text = item.get('Matn') or item.get('hadithTxt') or ''
    # hadithTxt often contains metadata and brackets; but still store

query_full = "وَإِنْ تَسْكُنُوا إِلَى الْأَرْضِ فَاسْأَلُوا مَا أَسْكَنَتْكُمْ"
query_norm = normalize_arabic(query_full)
query_norm

from difflib import SequenceMatcher
def token_f1(a, b):
    ta = a.split()
    tb = b.split()
    ca = Counter(ta)
    cb = Counter(tb)
    overlap = sum(min(ca[t], cb[t]) for t in set(ca.keys()) & set(cb.keys()))
    if len(ta)==0 or len(tb)==0:
        return 0.0
    precision = overlap / len(ta)
    recall = overlap / len(tb)
    if precision+recall == 0:
        return 0.0
    return 2*precision*recall/(precision+recall)

def best_matches(norm_list, qnorm, topk=10):
    scores = []
    for i, norm, text in norm_list:
        seq = SequenceMatcher(None, qnorm, norm).ratio()
        t_f1 = token_f1(qnorm, norm)
        # lcs length normalized maybe use SequenceMatcher.find_longest_match
        sm = SequenceMatcher(None, qnorm, norm)
        lcs_len = sm.find_longest_match(0,len(qnorm),0,len(norm)).size
        lcs_ratio = lcs_len / max(len(norm),1)
        # combine score - maybe weighted
        combined = max(seq, t_f1, lcs_ratio)
        scores.append((combined, seq, t_f1, lcs_ratio, i, norm, text))
    scores.sort(reverse=True, key=lambda x: x[0])
    return scores[:topk]

top_q = best_matches(q_norms, query_norm, topk=20)
top_q[:10]

seq, t_f1, lcs_len, lcs_ratio
